provenance.source_corpus: Reclaimed soils, fertilizer, and bioavailable nutrients: Determining similarity with natural benchmarks over time

source_spans.text: Fertilization increased similarity in nutrient supply profiles between reclaimed and benchmark soils only in 2012
Fertilization increased similarity in nutrient supply profiles between reclaimed and benchmark soils only in 2012
Total inorganic nitrogen was increased by 1 –2 orders of magnitude for both soil types in 2012
A small but significant overall increase in P supply rate was found in both reclaimed soils after fertilization
A small but significant overall increase in P supply rate was found in both reclaimed soils after fertilization and K supply rate in only in fertilized FFMM
an increase in variability was detected in fertilized soils

nodes.name: Temporal trajectory of nutrient profile in PMM

source_spans.text: Fertilization aligned the temporal trajectory of the nutrient profile in PMM similar to benchmark conditions
Soil moisture appeared as a significant factor along with the annual variability for the supply rate of the key macronutrients except for TIN in reclaimed sites
Soil moisture appeared as a significant factor along with the annual variability for the supply rate of the key macronutrients except for TIN in reclaimed sites
